cuts.name: Rinds-Entrecôte
notes: Salt generously (flaky) 45+ min before (dry-brine) or right before grilling — never in the 10–40 min window. [SE]
Do not press the steak. Do not move it until it releases from the grate (~90 s on each first contact).
Crosshatch grill marks: rotate 45° halfway through each side. Cosmetic only.

cuts.name: Rinds-Filet
notes: Brush with neutral oil, salt, cracked black pepper. Butter baste in last 60 s optional.
Rest loosely tented — never cut early, all the juice is in there under light pressure.

cuts.name: Hohrücken (Rib steak, bone-in) / Rinds-Ribeye bone-in
notes: Render the fat cap first: sear fat-side down 60 s before the main sear.
Pull 2 °C earlier if the bone is thick — it keeps cooking aggressively during rest.

cuts.name: T-Bone / Porterhouse
notes: Stand on the bone edge for 60 s before plating to finish the filet side if it lags.
Rest on a cutting board, not a plate (plate traps steam under the bone).

cuts.name: Flank steak
notes: Marinades (lime, soy, chipotle, chimichurri) love this cut.
Slice in ~5 mm strips across the grain — identify the grain direction before cooking, it's obvious.
Never past medium — collagen doesn't have time to render in a thin steak.

cuts.name: Skirt steak
notes: Trim outer silverskin.
Pat dry — the exterior crust is the whole point.
Across the grain, thin slices, same as flank.

cuts.name: Tri-tip
notes: Pull at 52 °C for medium-rare (carryover is real on a 1 kg piece).
Slice thin, watch the grain change at the apex — re-orient for the second half of the roast.

cuts.name: Sirloin
notes: Score the fat strip in 3 places before grilling so it doesn't curl the steak.
Sear the fat strip standing on edge 45–60 s at the end.

cuts.name: Roastbeef
notes: Pull from indirect at 48–50 °C internal. Rest 10 min while grill rips to max. Sear all sides. Rest again 15+ min.
Cut across the grain, 3–5 mm slices.

cuts.name: Kalbs-Hohrücken
notes: Oil and salt only; pepper after cooking (burns easily on veal).
Butter baste in last 30 s with thyme/garlic — classic.

cuts.name: Kalbs-Kotelett
notes: Stand on the bone 60 s at end for flavor infusion.
Finish at 60 °C pull → 63 °C after rest.

cuts.name: Schweinsteak / Pork steak
notes: Pork fat seasons the grate; watch flare-ups, move to indirect if needed.
Marinated versions: reduce heat to 200 °C — sugar burns.

cuts.name: Schweinsfilet
notes: Remove silverskin before grilling — doesn't dissolve, gets leathery.
Brine for 30 min in 5 % salt solution if you have time; fantastic.

cuts.name: Schweinshals
notes: Marinated Schweinshals (the classic Grillgut): start on direct medium-low (180–200 °C) to avoid burning the marinade, finish on direct high 30–60 s/side for color.
Flip every 60 s if marinade is sugar-heavy.

cuts.name: Schweinskoteletts
notes: Brine 30 min (5 % salt) for a margin of safety.
Stand on bone-edge 60 s at end.

cuts.name: Spareribs
notes: Remove membrane from bone side (use a paper towel for grip).
Bend test: lift one end with tongs, crack should form on the surface when meat is done.
Glaze only in the last 20–30 min — sauce sugar burns.

cuts.name: Lamm-Koteletts
notes: Trim excess fat to prevent flare-ups.
Season with rosemary + garlic + olive oil 30 min before.

cuts.name: Lamm-Rack
notes: Score the fat cap in diamonds before cooking.
Cover exposed bones with foil to avoid burning.

cuts.name: Lamm-Gigot
notes: Studs of garlic + rosemary in the meat — classic.
Butterflied is faster and easier to cook evenly.

cuts.name: Pferdesteak
notes: Do NOT cook past medium-rare — horse is ~ 3 % fat and will brick.
Marinate in red wine + juniper + bay leaf for flavor depth.

cuts.name: Pouletschnitzel
notes: Dry-brine (1 % salt) for 1–24 h — enormous texture payoff.
Pound to even thickness or butterfly thick breasts.

cuts.name: Pouletschenkel / Pouletoberschenkel
notes: Thighs cook to 80–85 °C — collagen rendering is the whole point. Don't stop at 72 °C, it'll be rubbery.
Skin-side down first, don't move until it releases.

cuts.name: Poulet-Flügeli
notes: 1 tsp baking powder per 500 g wings raises skin pH and drastically improves crisp.
Sauce AFTER cooking; sauce on grill = carbonized goop.

cuts.name: Whole butterflied chicken
notes: Remove backbone with kitchen shears, press flat — halves the cook time vs whole roasted.
Dry-brine 12–24 h in fridge, uncovered last 2 h for skin.

cuts.name: Pouletspiessli
notes: Soak bamboo skewers 30 min.
Leave small gap between pieces for even cooking.

cuts.name: Entenbrust
notes: Score fat in diamonds but don't cut into meat.
Start on a cool zone so fat renders before skin chars.
Pour off fat for confit potatoes — never waste it.

cuts.name: Trutenschnitzel
notes: Brine or marinate — dry turkey breast is unforgiving.

cuts.name: Cervelat
notes: It's pre-cooked — internal temp doesn't matter, "hot through + color" is the goal.
Keep lid open for better char control.

cuts.name: Grillschnecke / Bratwurstschnecke
notes: Pierce skewers at 90° angle to hold the coil flat — otherwise it unravels.

cuts.name: Kalbsbratwurst
notes: No piercing, no mustard (traditionalist position; Migusto hedges). No splitting.
Keep heat moderate — casing bursts above 230 °C easily.

cuts.name: BBQ-Speck
notes: Bacon on direct flame = grease fire. Always perforated foil or a grill plate.

cuts.name: Hamburger
notes: Cheese on 30 s before pulling, close lid to melt.

USDA and Swiss food safety advise 70 °C for ground beef because grinding redistributes surface bacteria throughout. Medium-rare on whole-muscle-ground-at-home is a different risk profile. Grillmi should default to 70 °C pull for any ground meat UI and offer a "home-ground only" override.

cuts.name: Lachsfilet
notes: Dry the skin hard with paper towels before oiling — crispy skin depends on it.
Don't move it for the first 3 min on skin side — it will release when ready.

cuts.name: Lachs
notes: Don't flip a side — just lift onto a serving board from the skin.
Plank version adds ~5 min.

cuts.name: Forelle
notes: Use a fish basket — trout skin is delicate and tears easily.
Stuff with lemon + herbs.

cuts.name: Dorade / Branzino
notes: Score skin in 3 diagonal cuts on each side — heat penetrates, skin doesn't curl.
Salt cavity, stuff with lemon slices, herbs.

cuts.name: Gelbflossenthunfisch
notes: Roll edges on the grate 15 s each for full crust coverage.

cuts.name: Schwertfisch
notes: Very forgiving for a fish — dense, meaty texture.

cuts.name: Riesencrevetten
notes: "C" shape = perfect. Tight "O" = overcooked.
Skewer 2 parallel skewers per 3–4 prawns to prevent spinning.

cuts.name: Aubergine
notes: Optional: salt, drain 15 min, pat dry (removes bitterness, but modern eggplant rarely needs it).

cuts.name: Champignons
notes: Don't wash — wipe with damp cloth; water makes them steam not sear.

cuts.name: Portobello
notes: Fill the gill side with cheese / herbs at the last 2 min.

rows.prepLabel: Whole head, top trimmed, oiled, foil

cuts.name: Pak Choi
notes: Oil + soy sauce brush; watch, it goes from char to burn in 30 s.

cuts.name: Spargeln
notes: White asparagus — peel, parboil 3 min first, then grill indirect 8–10 min wrapped in foil with butter.

cuts.name: Tofu
notes: Oil heavily — tofu will stick. Use a clean hot grate.
Marinate after pressing, not before.

cuts.name: Plant-based burger patty
notes: Do NOT pre-thaw aggressively — cook from fridge-cold.
Don't press; they release fat and dry out.
No internal temp guidance — cook until hot through (65–70 °C core).

cuts.name: Avocado
notes: Only the cut side gets grill marks; you don't flip.
Must be perfectly ripe — under-ripe tastes of nothing, over-ripe falls apart.

rows.prepLabel: In skin, slit lengthwise, stuffed with chocolate

cuts.name: Feigen
notes: Balsamic glaze + honey in last 30 s.

cuts.name: Marroni

rows.prepLabel: Whole, X cut on flat side, soaked 15 min

notes: The X cut is mandatory — un-scored chestnuts explode.
Peel while warm; cold, the skin sticks.

cuts.name: Wassermelone
notes: Dry slices thoroughly with paper towels; watermelon is mostly water and needs HOT grate to mark.

cuts.name: Zitrone / Limette
notes: Squeezes ~50 % more juice once grilled; citrus sugars caramelize for a completely different flavor.
